PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS INSTRUCTED BY COURT VIA TELEPHONE TO SERVE/FILE NOTICE OF RE-SET HEARING DATE/TIME ON DEFENDANT - HEARING SET FOR FEB-01-2021 AT 01:30 PM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-19-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF 3049 24TH STREET LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFFS FIRST SET OF ADMISSION PROPOUNDED TO DEFENDANT AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST ELVIS MAZARIEGOS, THE COURT RESETS TODAY'S HEARING SO THAT A SPANISH-LANGUAGE INTERPRETER MAY BE PRESENT FOR DEFENDANT AT THE RE-SET HEARING. THE CLERK CONFIRMS 2/1/21 AT 1:30 P.M. WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM FOR THE RE-SET HEARING. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS INSTRUCTED TO SERVE/FILE NOTICE OF THE RE-SET HEARING ON THE DEFENDANT. (302/JPT)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2023 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR #13668, 209-747-1306, briannarudd@gmail.com

Plaintiff 3049 24th Street, LLC represented by Lisa Padilla, Esq. and Justin Goodman, Esq.
Defendant Elvis Mazariegos appearing in pro per. Defendant is assisted by a Spanish Language Interpreter, Marissa Ayerdi; Certification No. 300927.

JERIES AZAR is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

DILCIA SANCHEZ, EDIS SANCHEZ, and JUAN GOMEZ are sworn to testify on behalf of Defendant.

Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.
Court recites summary of case as set forth on the record.

Plaintiff's counsel describes the property at issue.

Defendant is in default status.
Court takes judicial notice of Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion for order Deeming Admitted Truth of Matters Specified in Plaintiff's First Set of Requests for Admission Propounded to Defendant and for Monetary Sanctions filed Feb-02-2021. Twenty-Eight requests for admissions deemed true.

Court reads through admissions to determine the Court's boundaries.
Court and Plaintiff's counsel confer re: proposed judgment and Plaintiff's counsel presents offer of proof.

JERIES AZAR is examined by the Court. Witness attests that statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint and Defendant's admissions ordered to be true.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff 3049 24th Street, LLC, and against Elvis Mazariegos, as follows:

1. Defendant Mazariegos is the only original occupant of the Premises; and
2. Defendant Mazariegos is not permanently residing at the Premises known as 3053 24th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94110; and
3. Defendant Mazariegos is permanently enjoined from charging and receiving from DOES 1 -7 a disproportionate share of the total rent paid to Plaintiff and from profiting from the subleasing in violation of the Rent Ordinance; and
4. Restitution of the captured gain in the amount of $9,000.00 computed as follows:
Defendant was overpaid a total of $150.00 per month for five years totaling $9,000.00.
5. Plaintiff is awarded costs of suit incurred in the sum of $1,160.93 for a total judgment of $10,160.93.

Proposed judgment to be submitted.

Off Calendar Jan-12-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:28:32 a.m. and concluding at 09:41:22 a.m.

Plaintiff 3049 24th Street LLC, a California Limited Liability Company represented by Lisa Padilla, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Elvis Mazariegos appearing in pro per. Defendant is assisted by a Spanish Language Interpreter Shannon Raintree; Certification No. 300563.

JERIES AZAR is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Defendant Elvis Mazariegos is present in court.
Default entered as to Defendant on Aug-21-2020.

Court cannot determine if Defendant was properly served.
Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel regarding proof of service of its request for default judgment and notice of today's hearing.

Court finds there is no evidence that Defendant was properly served and noticed for today's hearing.
Court orders matter continued to Mar-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for Plaintiff to properly serve Defendant and notice it for new hearing.

MINI MINUTES:

Lisa Padilla (415-956-8100; lisa@zfplaw.com) of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC for Plaintiff.

Stipulation from 1/19/2021 on file.

Discussed (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript). The court noted defendant waived his right as he did not properly appear in the case and has not filed any oppositions. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Re-Set Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiffs First Set Of Admission Propounded To Defendant And For Monetary Sanctions Against Elvis Mazariegos: GRANTED.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiffs First Set Of Admission Propounded To Defendant And For Monetary Sanctions Against Elvis Mazariegos
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LISA K. PADILLA, Esq. of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC (Tel: 415-956-8100) for plaintiff 3049 24th STREET LLC;
Defendant ELVIS MAZARIEGOS present in pro per.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF 3049 24TH STREET LLC's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiffs First Set Of Admission Propounded To Defendant And For Monetary Sanctions Against Elvis Mazariegos.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiff authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Defendant indicates a need for a Spanish-language interpreter. A court Spanish-language interpreter is not currently present or available to assist.

The Court indicates it is inclined to grant plaintiff's motion as unopposed and sign a proposed order if one is submitted by plaintiff, but will first confirm with Dept. 302 as to whether the Court can do so now without an interpreter being present today.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported.

-----

**** After the hearing, at the direction of the Judge Pro Tem, the clerk contacts plaintiff's counsel to indicate that the court is resetting today's hearing so that a Spanish-language interpreter may be present for defendant at the re-set hearing. The clerk confirms 2/1/21 at 1:30 p.m. with plaintiff's counsel and the Judge Pro Tem for the re-set hearing. Plaintiff's counsel is instructed to serve/file notice of the re-set hearing on the defendant. (302/JPT)
